title: source_refs
description: Shared schema fragment for source_refs arrays used by every canonical template spec. A source_refs array is parallel to a values array: source_refs[i] describes where values[i] came from in the source workbook. This schema defines the allowed forms for a single source_refs element; it also defines a reusable 'array' form for the common parallel-array case.

cellAddress.description: An absolute-column, absolute-row Excel cell address without sheet prefix (e.g., 'D42'). The sheet is inherited from the spec's source_sheet_default.

rangeAddress.description: An Excel range address without sheet prefix (e.g., 'D42:D45').

cellRef.description: Explicit cell reference with sheet and cell address.

rangeRef.description: Range reference with an aggregator. Used when a single spec value represents a sum, mean, or other aggregation over multiple workbook cells (e.g., a subtotal row).

aggregator.description: How the audit should reduce the range to a single comparable value.

sourceRef.description: A single source_refs element. One of: null (value is computed from others in the spec), a bare cell address string (uses source_sheet_default), an explicit cell reference object, or a range reference with aggregator. Formula references are intentionally not supported: the audit compares evaluated values only.

oneOf.description: Value is computed from others in the spec (e.g., a growth rate derived from two adjacent revenue values). The audit validates by recomputation, not by workbook lookup.

sourceRefArray.description: A parallel array of source_refs, one per value in the associated values array. Must have the same length as the values array it parallels.
